title: salix × friesiana
intro: Salix × friesiana, also known as the hybrid willow or Fries' willow, is a cross between the white willow (Salix alba) and the weeping willow (Salix babylonica). It is a deciduous shrub or small tree that has been used for its medicinal properties for centuries. The bark and leaves of Salix × friesiana contain salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which provides pain relief, reduces inflammation, and has antiseptic properties. The herbal preparations of Salix × friesiana include tea, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including headaches, fever, and skin conditions.
